rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW PERCIVAL, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF VS. WALGREEN CO, DBA WALGREENS AN ILLINOIS CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 6, 2011, line 3, PLAINTIFF MATTHEW PERCIVAL, Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. TO THE EXTENT DEFENDANT ACTUALLY SEEKS TO WITHHOLD SOMETHING ON THE BASIS OF PRIVILEGE, DEFENDANT MUST PROVIDE A PRIVILEGE LOG. THE PARTIES ARE DIRECTED TO MEET & CONFER RE: A PROTECTIVE ORDER. IF THEY CANNOT AGREE ON THE TERMS, EACH PARTY MUST SUBMIT A PROPOSED PROTECTIVE ORDER FOR THE COURT'S CONSIDERATION. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND YOUNG, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA RAYMOND YOUNG VS. HEART OF THE CITY FARMERS' MARKET, A NONPROFIT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: An Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction (Per Ex Parte Order Filed On 08-02-11)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 6, 2011, line 9, Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction. PLAINTIFF'S PETITION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE A LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING. PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROVE THE BREACH OF CONTRACT OR PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL. PLAINTIFF'S CITATION TO PARAGRAPH 27 OF THE RULES IS MISPLACED. THAT PARAGRAPH PERTAINS TO ENFORCEMENT OF MARKET RULES AND DISCIPLINE. THIS CASE INVOLVES A POLICY CHANGE NOT DISCIPLINE. PLAINTIFF ALSO DOES NOT SUFFICIENTLY EXPLAIN THE REASON FOR A DELAY IN SEEKING RELIEF AT THIS TIME. GRANTING THE INJUNCTION WOULD ACTUALLY CHANGE, NOT PRESERVE, THE STATUS QUO. =(302/LMG)
